BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The sometimes strong wind has transported some snow. Over a wide area wind slabs formed. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on wind-loaded slopes of all aspects above approximately 1600 m and in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Slab avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: As a consequence of the moderate to strong northeasterly wind, fresh snow drift accumulations formed. The wind slabs are poorly bonded with the old snowpack. They are to be assessed with care and prudence.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow will fall until the afternoon.
The fresh snow as well as all the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: The older wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering in particular on very steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise.
Backcountry touring calls for careful route selection.
On very steep slopes medium-sized and, in isolated cases, large natural avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and wind.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall on Sunday. Especially in the areas bordering Piedmont. The wind will be light to moderate adjacent to ridgelines.
Above approximately 900 m snow will fall.
Over a wide area new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack. Or on crusts.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is weak in some cases; its surface consists of faceted crystals. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of new snow and wind the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase from the early morning.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Some snow will fall until the afternoon.
The fresh snow as well as all the wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in some cases.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls: The older wind slabs remain for the foreseeable future prone to triggering in particular on very steep shady slopes. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise.
Backcountry touring calls for careful route selection.
On very steep slopes small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible as a consequence of new snow and wind.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
10 to 30 cm of snow will fall on Sunday. The wind will be light to moderate adjacent to ridgelines.
Above approximately 900 m snow will fall.
Over a wide area new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack. Or on crusts.
Towards its surface, the snowpack is weak in some cases; its surface consists of faceted crystals. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: Over a wide area snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather conditions will facilitate a gradual strengthening of the near-surface layers in particular on sunny slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: In some localities in some localities 10 cm of snow will fall. The small quantity of fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. Wind slabs represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Down to 1200 m snow will fall in some localities. The wind slabs of last week represent the main danger. The wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects. They can be released very easily. These have formed especially in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain. Weak layers exist in the old snowpack on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes. Avalanches can be triggered in the old snowpack reach large size in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
Snow sport activities outside marked and open pistes call for experience and great restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The fresh snow of last week and in particular the wind slabs formed by the strong wind are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
The strong wind has transported the new snow significantly. Individual weak layers exist in the old snowpack especially on shady slopes. Avalanches can in some places be released in deep layers in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence and size of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
20 to 30 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2000 m. The new snow is bonding poorly with the old snowpack in many places. The fresh and older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise. In particular on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach large size. This applies in particular at intermediate and high altitudes.
Backcountry touring calls for careful route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 1000 m snow will fall.
In particular low and intermediate altitudes, steep sunny slopes: The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: Over a wide area new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: As a consequence of the new snow the prevalence of the avalanche prone locations will increase.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
10 to 20 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall above approximately 2000 m. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise. In particular on steep shady slopes avalanches can be released in the old snow and reach large size in isolated cases. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example at high altitudes and in high Alpine regions. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released, even by a single winter sport participant.
Backcountry touring calls for careful route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 900 m snow will fall.
In particular low and intermediate altitudes, steep sunny slopes: The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In some cases new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Slab avalanches and dry loose snow avalanches are the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As a consequence of a moderate to strong wind, sometimes deep wind slabs formed on Wednesday adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. 10 to 15 cm of snow will fall above approximately 1500 m. The avalanche prone locations are covered with new snow and are difficult to recognise. The fresh and older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in isolated cases on steep shady slopes above approximately 2000 m. This applies in particular at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Above approximately 700 m snow will fall.
In particular low and intermediate altitudes, steep sunny slopes: The new snow is lying on top of a quite favourable old snowpack.
High altitudes and the high Alpine regions: In some cases new snow is lying on the soft surface of an old snowpack. In particular at elevated altitudes snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow are to be assessed with care and prudence.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of new snow and wind, mostly small wind slabs will form during the course of the night. Even single winter sport participants can release avalanches in some places. These can in some cases be triggered in near-surface layers and reach medium size. Caution is to be exercised in particular on very steep shady slopes above approximately 2200 m, as well as on very steep west and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m, in isolated cases also on very steep southeast, south and southwest facing slopes in high Alpine regions. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and adjacent to ridgelines.
Isolated whumpfing sounds can indicate the danger. Remotely triggered avalanches are possible in isolated cases.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can be triggered in near-ground layers. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular on very steep west, north and east facing slopes above approximately 2200 m. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable. Avalanches can in isolated cases reach large size.

Moist loose snow slides are possible as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night.

Wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, also on very steep west and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep southeast, south and southwest facing slopes in high Alpine regions.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack.

Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs can be released easily in some places, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, also on very steep west and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep southeast, south and southwest facing slopes in high Alpine regions. Avalanche prone locations are to be found in gullies and bowls, and behind abrupt changes in the terrain and adjacent to ridgelines. They are clearly recognisable to the trained eye. Small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized avalanches are possible.

Additionally in isolated cases avalanches can be triggered in near-ground layers. Such avalanche prone locations are rare but are barely recognisable. Especially transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack are unfavourable.

Moist loose snow slides are possible as the day progresses.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Up to 10 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night.

Wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack, especially on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes above approximately 2200 m, also on very steep west and east facing slopes above approximately 2500 m, as well as on very steep southeast, south and southwest facing slopes in high Alpine regions.

Avalanche prone weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack.

Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack. In the event of solar radiation this applies in particular.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: At elevated altitudes the avalanche prone locations are more prevalent and larger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The wind slabs of the last few days remain in some cases prone to triggering. They must be evaluated with care and prudence. The avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular at the base of rock walls, and adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls. Avalanches can in many places be released, mostly by large loads. Restraint should be exercised because avalanches can sweep people along and give rise to falls. Backcountry touring calls for meticulous route selection.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: At low and mid-mountain altitudes, the various phases of melting and refreezing have generally favored the formation of a hard, load-bearing surface crust that may become wetter during the day. At higher altitudes, the snowpack is more variable, as these crusts, often load-bearing, alternate with localized accumulations of wind-drifted snow, which are generally clearly visible. Weak layers exist in the snowpack in particular on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: The weather will be partly cloudy.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects.
Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh and older wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in particular on northwest to north to northeast facing aspects above approximately 2000 m. Avalanches can in isolated cases be released in the old snowpack and reach medium size on very steep northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and, in isolated cases, medium-sized moist loose snow avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 10 to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The new snow will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from variable directions, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.
Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights:
The fresh snow and the wind slabs must be evaluated with care and prudence in all aspects.
Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
The fresh and older wind slabs can be released by a single winter sport participant in particular on west to north to east facing aspects above approximately 2000 m. Avalanches can in isolated cases release deeper layers of the snowpack and reach medium size.
Weakly bonded old snow requires caution. These avalanche prone locations are to be found in particular in northwest to north to northeast facing aspects. Caution is to be exercised in particular on steep shady slopes, as well as at transitions from a shallow to a deep snowpack, when entering gullies and bowls for example.
As a consequence of warming during the day and solar radiation small and medium-sized natural avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
In some localities 15 to 25 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall. The new snow will be deposited on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.

As a consequence of a sometimes strong wind from variable directions, sometimes avalanche prone wind slabs formed in the last few days. The fresh and somewhat older wind slabs are lying on the unfavourable surface of an old snowpack.
Distinct weak layers exist in the bottom section of the snowpack in particular on steep shady slopes. This applies especially above approximately 2000 m.

Steep sunny slopes: Sunshine and high temperatures will give rise to moistening of the snowpack.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Fresh wind slabs are in some cases still prone to triggering in particular on steep shady slopes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wind slabs and wet snow represent the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of warming during the day individual small to medium-sized wet avalanches are possible above approximately 1300 m.
In the vicinity of peaks a dangerous avalanche situation will be encountered in some localities. The more recent wind slabs are easy to recognise and can be released easily.
Avalanches can penetrate down to the ground and reach medium size in some cases.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The solar radiation will give rise as the day progresses to rapid moistening of the snowpack.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: The wind slabs represent the main danger. Weak layers in the old snowpack necessitate caution and restraint.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
As a consequence of a strong wind from easterly directions, sometimes deep wind slabs formed in the last two days. These are to be found adjacent to ridgelines and in gullies and bowls and generally at elevated altitudes. Avalanches can in some places be released, even by a single winter sport participant. Mostly they are medium-sized.
Up to 15 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall in the next few hours above approximately 1500 m. The wind slabs will be covered with new snow and therefore difficult to recognise. Mostly small natural avalanches are possible in the regions exposed to precipitation.
Distinct weak layers exist deep in the snowpack on shady slopes. Weak layers in the old snowpack can be released in some places by individual winter sport participants especially here. Whumpfing sounds serve as an alarm indicating the danger. Avalanches can in very isolated cases be triggered in the old snowpack and reach quite a large size.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
The weather conditions facilitated a gradual settling of the snowpack.
Especially adjacent to ridgelines in all aspects: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. Here snow depths vary greatly, depending on the infuence of the wind.
Distinct weak layers exist deep in the snowpack on shady slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.tendencyComment: Up to 5 cm of snow, and even more in some localities, will fall during the night. Wind slabs and weakly bonded old snow require caution.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: Wet snow represents the main danger.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: As the day progresses as a consequence of warming during the day there will be an increase in the danger of wet avalanches. Above approximately 1300 m small moist and wet avalanches are possible.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The snowpack will be in most cases moist.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment:
Dry avalanches can be released, in particular by large loads, but they will be small in most cases. Individual avalanche prone locations are to be found on steep shady slopes at high altitude.
As a consequence of warming during the day only isolated moist snow slides and avalanches are possible, but they will be mostly small, in the event of solar radiation in particular on extremely steep slopes.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment:
Evening and night: Some snow will fall in some localities.

Shady slopes: Isolated avalanche prone weak layers exist in the old snowpack in particular on northwest, north and northeast facing slopes.

Sunny slopes: The old snowpack will be moist. The weather conditions will give rise to thorough wetting of the snowpack at intermediate and high altitudes.

BulletinMeasurements.avActivityHighlights: New snow and wind slabs above approximately 1000 m.
BulletinMeasurements.avActivityComment: The sometimes strong wind has transported the new snow. In some places wind slabs formed.
BulletinMeasurements.snowpackStructureComment: The wind slabs represent the main danger.
